For you, AI isn’t an add-on, it’s the default operating system for how you think, work, and lead. You’re relentlessly curious about new capabilities, and you push the limits of what’s possible, turning AI into a competitive advantage for yourself, Upsales, and our customers. This is a role for someone with real ambition: someone who wants to set a new standard for efficiency, precision, and impact in the age of AI.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Drive end-to-end new business. From prospecting to negotiation and close.
* Create and execute strategic outbound campaigns toward key verticals and segments.
* Engage with CEOs, CROs, and other revenue leaders to identify growth opportunities.
* Position Upsales AI as a must-have platform for data-driven sales and marketing teams.
* Collaborate with product, marketing, and solution engineers to craft winning proposals.
* Build a strong, predictable pipeline, and consistently exceed your targets.


**Who We're Looking For**
 This isn’t an entry-level role, we’re looking for someone who has already
 **proven they can win at the highest level** 
 .
   

  

* At least 5 years of proven experience in complex B2B sales
* A degree from a respected academic institution, preferably in the technical or economic field
* A strong track record of meeting and exceeding targets
* Experience building long-term relationships with fast-growing mid-sized companies
* A proactive, ambitious mindset with full ownership of the sales process
* Excellent communication skills in both English and Swedish
* Driven by growth, responsibility, and the ambition to build something meaningful


 Personality-wise? You’re
 **obsessed with winning** 
 , but never at the expense of the team. You take pride in delivering results, but you’re also the kind of person who jumps in to help a teammate close a deal or solve a tough problem. You raise the bar, and bring others with you.
   

  

 Read our Culture Playbook
   

  

 Upsales was founded in 2003, grew without a single outside investor, and has been profitable ever since. No VC pressure. No compromises. Just a product customers keep paying for.
   

  

 We're now in our highest growth phase in years, one of the very few SaaS companies growing profitably while staying genuinely ahead on AI.
   

  

 If you want real autonomy, colleagues who push you, and work that moves the needle from day one, you'll fit right in.

title: Enterprise Architect
description:
We are looking for an experienced Enterprise Architect / Domain Enterprise Architect for one of our clients within the construction industry. This is to strengthen our clients enterprise architecture capability within one or several business domains. The role focuses on aligning business strategy, capability development, information, processes, and technology in accordance with the companies architectural principles and target architecture.
   

  

**Start: ASAP**
**End: 2026-12-31**
**Location: Stockholm, min 3 days/week at the office**
 The assignment requires a senior architect who can operate across strategic, tactical, and operational levels, combining holistic enterprise thinking with hands-on architecture and design. The architect is expected to collaborate closely with business and IT stakeholders, guide architectural decision‑making, and ensure coherent evolution of the domain architecture over time.
   

  

**Key expectations include:**
* Understanding business needs and strategic objectives within a specific domain, and translating these into architectural direction, capability improvements, and structured demands.
* Fluent in Swedish and English Defining and maintaining current and target state architectures for the domain, including perspectives on processes, information, applications, and technology.
* Evaluating and qualifying demands and initiatives from an architectural perspective, assessing dependencies, feasibility, risks, and alignment with internal architecture principles and target architecture.
* Supporting roadmap and backlog planning, providing architectural recommendations on sequencing, prioritization, and scope of initiatives and change activities.
* Designing and governing conceptual and high‑level solution architectures, while coordinating with Solution Architects and delivery teams for more detailed design where needed.
* Participating in architectural governance forums (e.g. Service Design Forum / Committee, Data Domain Forums and Councils), ensuring that proposals and changes comply with agreed principles, standards, patterns, and guidelines.
* Providing architectural support to stakeholders, including Product Owners, Service Managers, Process Owners, and Business Experts, especially related to architectural models, dependencies, and impacts.
* Identifying architectural risks and deviations, and proactively guiding initiatives to mitigate those risks or realign with target architecture.
* Collaborating with other architects to ensure cross‑domain alignment and contribute to the coherent evolution of Internal overall enterprise architecture.


 The role requires a consultant who can navigate complexity and ambiguity, communicate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders, and balance strategic intent with pragmatic execution. The assignment is not limited to documentation but emphasizes active architectural leadership, decision support, and collaboration.
   

  

 An experienced Enterprise Architect with strong proven experience in the above-related areas, including solid experience from information architecture and modelling. Must be able to translate analytical and operational needs into structured, harmonized information models that support our journey in becoming data-informed This involves designing consistent data structures and ensuring alignment with internal strategic ambition for data availability.

title: Cloud Infrastructure Architect AWS
description:
Choosing Capgemini means choosing a company where you will be empowered to shape your career in the way you’d like, where you’ll be supported and inspired by a collaborative community of colleagues around the world, and where you’ll be able to reimagine what’s possible. Join us and help the world’s leading organizations unlock the value of technology and build a more sustainable, more inclusive world.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Vill du vara med och forma framtidens molnlösningar?
   

  

 Du blir en del av Capgeminis Cloud & Infrastructure Services (CIS)-enhet med fokus på projekt- och konsulttjänster i Norden. Vårt mål är att erbjuda kunder helhetslösningar inom Cloud och vi erbjuder en mängd olika tjänster till våra kunder, från strategisk rådgivning till projektgenomförande.
   

  

*I denna roll kommer du ha en nyckelroll i att:*
* Designa och implementera komplexa, skalbara och säkra AWS-lösningar anpassade efter kundens behov
* Ge teknisk vägledning och support vid felsökning under en hel projektleverans
* Samarbeta med intressenter för att samla in krav och föreslå effektiva migrationsstrategier
* Agera som en betrodd rådgivare till kunder gällande branschtrender och ny teknik
* Dela kunskap inom organisationen genom mentorskap, utbildning och skapande av återanvändbara artefakter


**Din Profil**
* Minst 7 års erfarenhet av praktisk design av molnbaserad företagsarkitektur, lagrings- och nätverkslösningar
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av att bygga, migrera och modernisera företagsapplikationer på enterprisenivå
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av molntransformation
* AWS-certifieringar på professionell nivå (t.ex. Solutions Architect Professional, DevOps Engineer Professional) samt erfarenhet av automatisering och skriptning (t.ex. Terraform, Python, CDK, shell, Ruby, Powershell)
* Kunskap om säkerhets- och efterlevnadsstandarder (t.ex. HIPAA, GDPR)
* Flytande i Svenska och engelska, både i tal och skrift


 Önskvärda kvalifikationer:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av AWS-tjänster (t.ex. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN), Cloud Migration Factory, Portfolio analysis tooling) samt hantering av storskaliga migreringar från datacenter till datacenter och/eller från datacenter till molnet; drift av datacenter (t.ex. datasäkring, övervakning, larm och notifieringar)
* Erfarenhet av att integrera AWS AI/ML-tjänster (SageMaker, Bedrock, Amazon Q); förståelse för styrning inom AI/ML och ansvarsfulla AI-praktiker


**Vad vi tror kommer få dig att trivas:**
* Konkurrenskraftig lön, förmånliga tjänstepensioner och kollektivavtal som ger trygghet
* Fokus på din hälsa och ditt välmående, med initiativ för att skapa balans mellan jobb och fritid
* En arbetsmiljö med kollegor som stöttar och inspirerar varandra, och chefer som har ditt bästa i fokus
* Brett utbud av karriärmöjligheter och tillgång till Capgeminis stora utbildningskatalog, särskilt inom den senaste teknologin för t.ex. cloud och generativ AI
* En företagskultur där mångfald och inkludering värdesätts högt


**Ansökan**
*I denna rekrytering hanterar vi ansökningar löpande. Som en del av vår rekryteringsprocess kommer identitetskontroll att genomföras genom granskning av ditt ID-kort, och bakgrundskontroller kan utföras vid behov. Vid frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Jessica Fogelsjöö, Jessica.fogelsjoo@capgemini.com.*
 Till denna roll söker vi medarbetare för säkerhetsklassat uppdrag placerad hos kund.
   

  

 Capgemini is a global business and technology transformation partner, helping organizations to accelerate their dual transition to a digital and sustainable world, while creating tangible impact for enterprises and society. It is a responsible and diverse group of 340,000 team members in more than 50 countries. With its strong over 55-year heritage, Capgemini is trusted by its clients to unlock the value of technology to address the entire breadth of their business needs. It delivers end-to-end services and solutions leveraging strengths from strategy and design to engineering, all fueled by its market leading capabilities in AI, generative AI, cloud and data, combined with its deep industry expertise and partner ecosystem.

title: CFO
description:
**About us** 




 We're a fast-growing startup, Delaware-incorporated and operating out of Stockholm. We just closed a seed round and are heads-down building toward Series A. Our product serves both consumers and businesses, with B2C currently the larger surface area. We move fast, ship constantly, and our investors expect the same rigor from our financial operations that we apply to our product.
 



**Why we're hiring** 




 We have set a clear bar: we need to operate like a Series A-ready company
 *before* 
 we start talking to Series A investors. That means clean books, defensible models, real KPIs, and a CFO who can sit across the table from VCs and speak their language.
 



 We have the scaffolding in place — a bookkeeping firm closing the books monthly, a KPI dashboard, drafted board governance documents, planned committee structures, reporting cycles. What we need now is the person who turns the scaffolding into operating reality and pushes the financial discipline of the company up several levels.
 



**What you'll own** 



* **Series A readiness.** 
 Build the financial story, model, and data room. Translate our traction into the language VCs underwrite against — unit economics, cohort behavior, CAC/LTV, payback, magic number, rule of 40, the works.
* **Financial planning & analysis.** 
 Own the budget, the rolling forecast, and the scenario models. Pressure-test every assumption.
* **Burn and runway management.** 
 Track burn rate, runway, and net burn. Question spend. Bring the CEO uncomfortable but useful answers before they're asked.
* **KPI integrity.** 
 We already have a dashboard. Your job is to make sure every number in it is true, consistently defined, and tied to something that matters. Be the auditor of our own metrics.
* **Board reporting.** 
 Prepare board materials, financial packages, and pre-reads. Own the reporting cadence.
* **Corporate governance.** 
 Enforce what's been drafted: board calendar, committee charters, resolutions, cap table hygiene, 409A valuations, option grant proposals, equity administration.
* **Bookkeeping oversight.** 
 Manage the relationship with our external bookkeeping firm, review the monthly close, ensure US GAAP compliance.
* **Cash and treasury.** 
 Banking relationships, FX exposure (SEK/USD/EUR), payment ops, vendor terms.
* **Investor relations.** 
 Frequent updates to existing investors, directly or through the CEO. Be the financial face of the company to current and prospective backers.
* **Operational finance.** 
 Procurement discipline, contract review with vendors, SaaS spend hygiene, headcount planning with the CEO.



**What you bring** 




**Must-haves** 



* Prior CFO experience at a venture-backed, fast-growing startup — and you took a company through a Series A or later round.
* **Hands-on experience with a Delaware C corp structure** 
 — especially one operating internationally. You know the difference between GAAP and IFRS.
* Fluency in SaaS / consumer subscription metrics and the specific way VCs read them. You should be able to build a defensible bottoms-up revenue model from a blank sheet.
* Comfort in a high-velocity startup environment. You make decisions with incomplete information, you don't need a team of analysts to ship a board deck, and you don't flinch at rolling up your sleeves on the actual spreadsheet.
* Strong communicator in English (written and verbal). Comfortable presenting to a board, pushing back on a CEO.
* Bring both an operational and strategic mindset to the CFO role and C-suite/board discussions supported by relevant financial analysis



**Nice-to-haves** 



* Experience operating a US/Swedish (or US/Nordic) corporate structure
* Background in B2C subscription and/or B2B SaaS, ideally a blend
* AI-native or AI-adjacent product experience (you understand how unit economics shift in an AI-cost-of-goods world)
* Network with Series A funds active in the US and EU ecosystem.



**What we're not looking for** 




 A pure career-controller who's never built a model from a blank page. A CFO who treats governance documents as the deliverable rather than the starting point. Anyone who needs us to explain why a SaaS company's gross margin is structurally different from a hardware company's.
 



  





**How to apply** 




  






 Send an email with your CV and a note on why you think you would be a good fit to filip.landgren@serus.ai.
 




  






 OTHER:
 





 This is a full time or part time position.

title: Senior Analyst - Internal Audit & Risk
description:
Spotify’s Internal Audit & Risk (IAR) team is seeking a Senior Analyst with a technological or engineering background to help implement our audit strategy across the product lifecycle within our agile, international environment. In this role you will be working on a diverse range of projects, from building tools leveraging AI agents to supporting the company's annual SOX compliance program and contributing to assurance and advisory projects across technology and product areas.
   

  

 The ideal candidate has an engineering background, good understanding of software development in microservices architecture, and has some experience in building AI systems and using AI powered coding assistants. You should be passionate about risk management and be keen on learning auditing techniques.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Test technology components within the SOX framework, including planning, executing walkthroughs, reporting, and driving continuous improvements such as control rationalization.
* Design, build, and iterate on internal audit & risk tooling and systems using AI agents, leveraging modern cloud-native architectures and generative AI models to drive execution.
* Champion technology-enabled audit execution by embedding AI directly into core audit processes to deliver deeper, more effective results.
* Develop a deep understanding of the businesses, products, and processes you are auditing, including their strategy, product lifecycle, and associated business processes.
* Participate in complex assurance and advisory projects, covering risk assessment, testing, and reporting across various technical domains.
* Coordinate with co-sourced providers, external auditors, and internal process owners to ensure the timely and effective execution of the SOX testing program and operational/technical audits.
* Provide guidance and support to internal stakeholders as they address control deficiencies or make significant process changes, such as the implementation of new systems or product features.


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have at least 3 years of work experience. The majority should come from an engineering role or similar in high performing, cross-functional teams. Experience in technology audit, IT consulting and/or IT risk management is a merit, but not a requirement.
* You effectively use AI agents in your day-to-day work, and you have at least basic experience in building AI systems or using AI powered coding assistants such as Claude Code or Cursor.
* You have a solid understanding of modern software development lifecycles (SDLC), programming languages for AI development (Python) and cloud infrastructure.
* You’re passionate about risk management and audit, and you’re eager to learn and grow your auditing skills under mentorship of our team of experts.
* You possess strong problem-solving skills, demonstrating critical and logical thinking to reach solutions in complex technical environments.
* You care deeply about quality and hold yourself and others to a high standard.
* You hold a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering or a related field. Self-taught engineers holding another degree are also welcome to apply. Professional certifications (e.g., CISA, CISSP, CIA) are a merit but not a requirement.


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in our New York City or Stockholm office. Remote work may be considered for strong candidates.
* We offer a hybrid work model, typically working 2–3 days per week in the office to foster collaboration with the IAR team and stakeholders.


 The United States base range for this position is $92,171 - $131,673 plus equity. The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, 13 paid flexible holidays, paid sick leave. These ranges may be modified in the future.
   

  

 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Head of Data Governance
description:
**Join our winning organization and passionate people!**
 At Orkla Snacks, our products are part of everyday life across the Nordics and beyond. From product development to shelf availability, it all depends on data that is accurate, consistent, and trusted.
   

  

 We are now looking for a Head of Data Governance to take ownership of how we define, protect, and govern data across the organization – setting the standards and structures that allow data to be a reliable asset for everyone who depends on it.
   

  

**Your role**
 You will be part of the IT leadership team, reporting to the IT Director, and lead our Data Governance function.
   

  

 Your responsibility is to build and maintain the frameworks, policies, and standards that define how data is owned, classified, and used across Orkla Snacks.
   

  

 You operate together with Head of Data, with the mandate to challenge, align, and set direction – across business units, platforms, and functions.
   

  

 It is a strategic ownership role for someone who understands that good governance is what makes data trustworthy at scale.
   

  

**What You Will Be Working On**
* Owning and developing the enterprise Data Governance framework – policies, principles, and standards
* Defining and enforcing data ownership structures across business units and domains
* Establishing metadata standards, data dictionaries, and business glossaries
* Setting data quality standards and the processes that uphold them
* Managing and evolving data classification and access policies, including compliance with regulatory requirements such as GDPR
* Driving adoption of governance practices into real business processes – not just documentation
* Working closely with Head of Data & Analytics to ensure governance frameworks support platform development and data product delivery
* Representing data governance in cross-functional forums and leadership discussions


**Who We Are Looking For**
 You have experience with Data and governance in a complex, international organization – and you know what it takes to make it work in practice. Experience from FMCG is an advantage.
   

  

**We believe you:**
* Have implemented governance structures across multiple business units
* Have experience with SAP and preferably tools such as SAP MDG or similar
* Understand how data connects to end-to-end business processes
* Have led and developed teams
* Communicate effectively in English


 As a person, you bring direction and structure, but also the ability to engage and align stakeholders across functions. You are comfortable driving change and focused on delivering results.
   

  

**Why join Orkla Snacks?**
 This is a role where you will shape how data is managed and used across an international business.
   

  

 You will work closely with both business and technology, influence key decisions, and help ensure that a solid data foundation supports everything from daily operations to future growth.
   

  

**Apply**
 If you are motivated by building structure, creating alignment, and making data work in practice, we would like to hear from you. Deadline: 24th May 2026
   

  

 Orkla Snacks is a market leader within confectionery, biscuits, and snacks across the Nordic and Baltic region. We operate in six markets and generate annual revenues of above
 **1** 
 0 billion NOK (2025).  Our company brings together more than 3,000 dedicated people, united by a passion for our strong brands that consumers love– including Smash!, OLW, BUBS, Panda, and Ballerina. We have strong #1 and #2 positions across most of our markets and categories.
   

  

 At our core, we bring joy to life’s many moments. We achieve this through a network of 12 factories, a strong local heritage, and a commitment to quality and sustainability.
   

  

 In 2025, Orkla Snacks was recognized on the TIME100 Most Influential Companies list, highlighting our impact and contribution to shaping the future of snacking.
   

  

 Our headquarters are located in Oslo, Norway.
   

  

 Orkla Snacks is an Orkla ASA portfolio company.

title: Systemutvecklare
description:
**Uppdragsbeskrivning:**
 I takt med att teknologin utvecklas och verksamhetens behov växer, genomför vi en uppgradering av vår nuvarande ARS-plattform till nästa version av BMC Helix. BMC Helix erbjuder moderna funktioner och möjligheter som kommer att förbättra användarupplevelsen, effektivisera processerna och möjliggöra en bättre förvaltning av våra applikationer. Syftet med detta migreringsprojekt är att säkerställa en smidig övergång till den nya plattformen, samt att anpassa och vidareutveckla applikationerna för att maximera deras funktionalitet och användbarhet. Vi söker en konsult som kommer att komplettera vårt team för att slutföra migreringen från ARS-plattformen till BMC Helix.
   

  

 Arbetet kommer att omfatta följande huvudområden:
   

  

* Konsulten ska arbeta med ett migreringsuppdrag från ARS-plattformen till nästa version av BMC Helix.
* Anpassning av applikationer inför migrering till Helix-plattformen.
* Förvalta och vidareutveckla applikationer på BMC Helix.
* Nyutveckling av applikationer och mobilversioner på BMC Helix.
* Versionshantering av Helix-plattformen


**Kvalificeringskrav**
* Minst ha nio (9) års dokumenterad erfarenhet av arbete som systemutvecklare.
* Minst fem (5) års erfarenhet av utveckling på ARS/Helix-plattformen med egenutvecklade applikationer, erfarenheten ska vara inhämtad under de senaste tio (10) åren.
* Erfarenhet av ARS versioner 7.1-20.02. Helix 25.3
* Minst fem (5) års erfarenhet av Transact-SQL.
* Minst fem (5) års erfarenhet av SSO.
* Minst fem (5) års erfarenhet av BMC Atrium Integrater Spoon.
* Konsulten ska ha god förmåga att uttrycka sig i tal och skrift på svenska.


**Meriterande kompetens**
* Minst tre års erfarenhet av arbetsprocesser inom it-utveckling på större myndighet. Erfarenheten ska vara inhämtad under de senaste tio åren.

title: Informationssäkerhetsspecialist
description:
Sök jobbet
   

  

 Har du erfarenhet av informationssäkerhet och vill bidra till säkerheten för Sveriges digitala utveckling inom hälsa, vård och omsorg? Lockas du av att vara med och driva säkerhetsarbetet i ett av de mest intressanta utvecklingsområdena i digitaliseringen av samhället? Då kan det här vara en roll för dig.
   

  

**Om tjänsten**
 Som informationssäkerhetsspecialist vid E-hälsomyndigheten kommer du att arbeta tillsammans med medarbetarna på enheten säkerhet- och beredskap, samt i nära samverkan med IT-säkerhetsansvarig, arkitekter, driftspersonal och verksamheten.
   

  

 Ditt ansvar är att stärka utvecklingen av myndighetens systematiska informationssäkerhet. Du har en förmåga att arbeta systematiskt med helhetsperspektiv, och utveckla styrande dokument och metodstöd, implementera strategier, arbetssätt och rutiner i hela organisationen samt följa upp att informationssäkerhetsarbetet stödjer myndighetens verksamhet och mål. Du tar även fram och genomför utbildningar samt utvecklar och deltar i myndighetens riskarbete med tillhörande analyser.
   

  

 Informationssäkerhet är en viktig och central del i kvalitetssäkringen av myndighetens uppdrag att stödja vård och hälsa genom digitala tjänster och lösningar.
   

  

 Dina arbetsuppgifter består av:
   

  

* Samordna, följa upp och utveckla E-hälsomyndighetens arbete med informationssäkerhet.
* Ta fram interna regelverk, metoder och processer utifrån lagkrav samt implementera dessa och följa upp efterlevnaden.
* Identifiera och bedöma hotbild och omsätta detta i interna krav och risker för verksamheten.
* Initiera riskbedömning för att identifiera och hantera hot och sårbarheter.
* Planera och genomföra målgruppsanpassade utbildningar, workshoppar eller andra kommunikativa insatser inom informationssäkerhetsområdet.
* Stödja verksamheten i bedömningar avseende informationssäkerhet.
* Omvärldsbevaka och följa utvecklingen inom området.


 Säkerhet och beredskap har uppdraget att styra, stödja och följa upp att myndigheten bedriver ett systematiskt och ändamålsenligt säkerhet- och beredskapsarbete. I detta ingår verksamhetsskydd, säkerhetsskydd, beredskap och uppgifter inom det civila försvaret.
   

  

**Om dig**
 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper och vi söker dig som är trygg och stabil och som har god förmåga att organisera ditt arbete. Du är flexibel och har förmågan att hantera omprioriteringar och är van att arbeta i grupp likväl som självständigt. I rollen kommer du att ha ett nära samarbete med dina kollegor och vi förutsätter att du har en god samarbetsförmåga och kan bygga förtroende. Du är analytisk, arbetar målinriktat och fokuserar på att nå uppsatta mål.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* relevant högskoleutbildning, eller annan utbildning i kombination med arbetslivserfarenhet som av arbetsgivaren bedöms som likvärdig
* minst tre års erfarenhet av informationssäkerhetsarbete
* erfarenhet av att analysera, revidera och implementera styrning, stöd och uppföljning av informationssäkerhet
* erfarenhet av att genomföra risk- och sårbarhetsanalyser samt kravställning vid upphandling/inköp.


**Det är meriterande för tjänsten om du har:**
* erfarenhet och förståelse för hur informationssäkerhetsarbetet inkluderat dataskydd kan integreras i säkerhets- och/eller kvalitetsledningssystem
* erfarenhet av informationssäkerhetsarbetet i agila team
* certifieringar som CISSP, CISM, CISA, eller liknande.


**Vad erbjuder vi?**
 E-hälsomyndigheten är en myndighet i tillväxt och vi driver utvecklingen av nationell digital infrastruktur inom hälsa, vård och omsorg. Vi skapar förutsättningar för digitala tjänster och lösningar för både privatpersoner och professioner inom hälso- och sjukvård, apotek och socialtjänst. Vi arbetar för att hälsodata ska bli tillgängliga i hela vårdkedjan.

title: Junior Sustainability Controller
description:
**Build with us - together with the best colleagues in the industry!**
 At NCC, we are driven by the desirefor development, whether it concerns our construction projects or our personal skills. Every day, we drive the industry’s most exciting and complex construction projects forward in close collaboration between our employees, partners, and customers. With us, you are a significant employee who contributes to the landmarks that define our cities and communities. Here, our employeestakean active role in a corporate culture founded on solid values and behaviors for a safe and secure workplace. Our combinedexpertiseand diverse backgrounds make a difference.
   

  

**NCC - one of the leading companies in the Nordics.**
 Are you a recent graduate with a degree in sustainability,economicsor similar field? Or do you have a few years of experience analyzing sustainability data, sustainability reporting, and are looking for the next challenge? Additionally, if you are a relationship builder with the desire and interest to drive change and development, NCC Industry offers an exciting role as a JuniorController with a sustainability profile. Here, you have great opportunities to develop both within the field of sustainability and personally, while helping us make data-driven decisions, navigate the green transition, and achieve our goal ofnet zero emissionsby 2045.
   

  

**Builda sustainability organization for the future.**
 The rapid changes in the sustainability landscape place high demands on us to measure, report, follow up, and document.We’rein abuild-up phase ofoursustainability organizationwhereyou’llget a unique opportunity tobe partfromthe beginning.This means that many structures, processes, and ways of working are still beingsetandyou’llhavea key role inour internal control andhow weleveragesustainability going forward.
   

  

 With curiosity, creativity, and a structured approach, you will be animportantasset tous atNCC.
   

  

**MainResponsibilities**
* Collection and analysis of sustainability-related data.
* Reportingand controlaccording to CSRD and the EU taxonomy.
* Monitoring sustainability goals and developing methods and processes.
* Compiling,visualizingand presenting sustainability data to relevantstakeholders within NCC, suppliers, and customers.


 You will be thecorefor sustainability reporting for us at NCC Industry and will have a wide network of contacts both within the business area, divisions Stone Materials and Asphalt, as well as across the group with corresponding roles from other business areas within the NCCGroup.
   

  

**About The Team**
 NCC Industry is a Nordic Organization that focuses on industrial production – stone material, asphalt, foundationworkand recycling of construction materials. We are a proactive partner with combinedexpertisein manufacturing and contracting with a strong focus on sustainable solutions.
   

  

 You are part of NCC Industry’s Nordic finance department andwill be part of a team withone otherLead Specialistand theHead of Sustainability, to whom you also report. You also collaborate closely withotherstafffunctionsin both the stone and asphalt operations.
   

  

**Your Experience And Background**
 Youhaveanengineering-oramaster’sdegree withinsustainability,oraneconomicdegreewithafocus on sustainability.Alternatively,you havefew years of experience in sustainability reporting,financialcontrolorclimate emissions reporting (GHG protocol).You have strong Excel skills, including experience with data analysis, calculations, and structured reporting.Additionally, experience with CSRD,theEUtaxonomy, ESG, environmental product declarations, or ISO 14001 isa big plus.
   

  

 We place great emphasis on your personal qualities and are looking for someone with strong analytical skills, who is solution-oriented andused to analyzinglargequantitiesofdata.You quickly absorb information, enjoy working in a changingenvironmentand contributing to development. To be successful in thisrolewe also see that you need to have aflexible approachandtheability todrive yourwork independently.
   

  

 Since the role has multiplepoints of contact, it is important that you haveexcellentcollaboration skills andthat buildingprofessionalrelationships comes naturally to you.
   

  

 It is keythat you arefluentinone of the Nordic languagesas well as in English, bothspokenandin writing.
   

  

**Otherinformation**
* Preferred location for thisis in Stockholm.
* Theposition may requiretravellingfrom time to time.
* This is a permanent position withan initialprobationary period of 6 months.
* In accordance withNCC: ssafety culture we conduct background checks on final candidates.


**NCC Offers**
 At NCC, we aim to be an attractiveemployer with employees who thrive, develop, and feel good.Therefore, we offer a wide range of employee benefits to those who work with us.NCC has collective agreements for all employees. This providesa goodbasic security and a clear set of rules so that you know what applies to your employment.We also offer excellent opportunities for continued development and a broad range of further education options, allowing fora long careerwithin the company.
   

  

**Contact and application**
 We are working continuously with the selection process, wekindly ask you to apply as soon as possible, but no later than2026-06-07.
   

  

 For further information on this position please contact recruiting managerPatrik Östberg,Head of Sustainabilityatpatrik.osterberg@ncc.se,orLinnea Stiernspetz, HR Business Partner, linnea.stiernspetz@ncc.se.
   

  

 Are you ready to make a significant impact and grow with us? Submit your application today and become an essential part of our innovativesustainabilityteam at NCC Industry!

title: SAP–Windchill Test Lead
description:
### **Assignment Description**


* Lead end-to-end testing activities for PTC Windchill implementations, upgrades, and enhancements.
* Define and own the test strategy, test plans, schedules, and entry/exit criteria.
* Manage system, integration, regression, performance, and user acceptance testing (UAT).
* Coordinate testing across Windchill modules (e.g., PDMLink, MPMLink, ProjectLink) and integrations with ERP, CAD (Creo), or other enterprise systems.
* Review and validate business and technical requirements to ensure test coverage.
* Design, review, and maintain test cases, test scenarios, and test data.
* Lead defect management including triage, prioritisation, root cause analysis, and retesting.
* Communicate test progress, risks, and quality metrics to stakeholders.
* Support UAT by guiding business users, managing test cycles, and ensuring signoff readiness.
* Drive quality best practices and continuous improvement within the testing lifecycle.


**Required Skills**


* Proven experience as a Test Lead or Senior Test Analyst.
* Hands-on experience testing PTC Windchill (configuration,workflows, lifecycle states, access control).
* Understanding of PLM business processes (Change Management, BOMs, Document Management).
* Strong knowledge of software testing methodologies and SDLC (Agile and/or Waterfall).
* Experience with test management and defect tracking tools (e.g. Azure DevOps, Jira, HP ALM).
* Excellent stakeholder communication and coordination skills.
* Experience testing Windchill upgrades, migrations, or cloud deployments.
* Exposure to CAD/PLM integrations (Inventor, Creo, SAP).

title: Infrastructure Engineer to T-Unit
company: ANTS Akademiskt Nätverk av Tekniska Studenter AB
description:
T-Unit
  
We are a recently well-funded, mission-driven startup building tools that help society withstand and adapt to security threats, natural disasters, extreme weather, and other risks. Our platform powers simulation, scenario planning, incident management, and early warning, enabling organizations to prepare for the unexpected.
  
  

About the Role
  
We are looking for an Infrastructure Engineer to join our team and help architect an AI-powered platform for crisis management, providing situational overviews, consequence modeling, and prioritization to support users in making critical decisions with real-world impact.
  
In this role, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (engineering, product, and operations) to build tools that save lives and protect property. You will also get the opportunity to contribute to the technical strategy, offering insights and solutions that align with the company’s goals. As a senior team member, you will be expected to mentor and guide peers, fostering a culture of learning and continuous improvement. Additionally, you will play a key role in assisting with hiring and building a strong, diverse team that thrives in a collaborative and inclusive environment.
  
  

**What You’ll Do:**  

* Manage and operate Kubernetes clusters, ensuring scalability and reliability.
* Work in a cloud-agnostic environment and sometimes on bare metal, deploying and maintaining critical infrastructure.
* Oversee S3 storage, and handle databases and backup operations, ensuring they are secure and resilient.
* Automate manual processes and implement monitoring solutions to maintain the platform’s performance and stability.


What You Bring
  
We understand that no one ticks every box, and that’s okay. If you're excited about the role and thrive in a collaborative environment, we'd love to hear from you. We are looking for someone who is comfortable working in cross-functional teams, eager to learn from others, and has a strong motivation to grow. If you are driven, humble, and have a growth mindset, this could be the perfect opportunity for you.
  
  

**Key Qualifications:**  

* 4+ years of experience in DevOps development.
* Expertise in Linux administration, Kubernetes (K8S), and scripting (e.g., Bash, Python)
* Strong skills in monitoring and/or automation
* Experience with on-prem environments
* Comfortable working in cross-functional teams and open to learning from others.


It’s a bonus if you have experience with backend development and familiarity with CEPH and S3. Additionally, it’s a bonus if you have experience with on-call work and handling high-pressure situations.
  
  

What We Offer
  
* Competitive compensation and long-term upside in a promising startup.
* A flexible work environment in an exciting, impactful domain.
* Support for professional development and continuous learning as the company grows.
* A team that values inclusion, empathy, and personal growth.


We welcome applicants of all backgrounds, identities, and experiences. If you need accommodation at any stage of the hiring process, please let us know. We’re happy to help. Background checks are part of our recruitment process.
  
  

If you have any questions about the role or the process, feel free to reach out to Axel Jeppsson at axel.jeppsson@ants.se

title: Applikationsspecialist
description:
*Vill du påverka och göra skillnad på riktigt? Här bidrar du till att skapa trygghet för människor och samhällen. Det du gör spelar roll.*


Din roll


Som Applikationsspecialist i vårt operationsteam arbetar du i gränslandet mellan 2nd och 3rd line och har en nyckelroll i drift och förvaltning av våra applikationer. Du säkerställer att systemen fungerar som de ska – idag och imorgon.


Rollen är tekniskt förankrad men innehåller också administrativa inslag såsom dokumentation, livscykelhantering och samordning kring applikationer, licenser och leverantörer. Vi arbetar idag med främst Atlassian-sviten, Doors, Jazz och Azure DevOps (TFS) och det ses som meriterande om du har vana att förvalta dessa verktyg.


Arbetsuppgifter

* Förvaltning, drift och felsökning av applikationer
* Ärendehantering på avancerad nivå (2nd/3rd line)
* Samarbete med utveckling, infrastruktur och våra leverantörer
* Dokumentation, rutiner och applikationsöversikt
* Säkerställa stabilitet, tillgänglighet och kvalitet i applikationsmiljön


Din profil


Vi söker dig som har en IT‑bakgrund, exempelvis inom systemadministration, IT‑support eller liknande. Du trivs med att arbeta strukturerat och noggrant och ser värdet i god administration – för dig är ordning, dokumentation och uppföljning en viktig och intressant del av en välfungerande IT‑organisation.


Du tycker om att lösa kluriga problem och drivs av att hjälpa andra människor, både genom tekniska lösningar och genom ett professionellt och serviceinriktat bemötande. Du har en stark problemlösningsförmåga, tar gärna ansvar för dina uppgifter och driver ärenden framåt på ett självständigt sätt.


Samtidigt är du kommunikativ och samarbetar väl med olika roller och funktioner inom organisationen. Har du dessutom erfarenhet av att arbeta nära 2nd och 3rd line support ser vi det som meriterande, då rollen innebär samverkan kring mer komplexa ärenden och tekniska lösningar.


Vi erbjuder

* Ett stabilt och kunnigt operations team med tydligt ansvar
* En varierad roll där teknik möter struktur och ordning
* Möjlighet att påverka och förbättra arbetssätt
* En viktig position i organisationens IT leverans


Vad du blir en del av


Upptäck oändliga möjligheter. Anta utmaningar, skapa smarta innovationer och uppgradera dina förmågor. Det här är en plats för kluriga tänkare, modiga pionjärer och alla däremellan. Tillsammans uppnår vi det extraordinära, var och en tar med sitt unika perspektiv. Varje bidrag räknas.


Saab är ett ledande försvars- och säkerhetsföretag med uppdrag att hjälpa nationer att skydda sin befolkning och bidrar till trygghet för människor och samhällen. Med 28.000 talangfulla medarbetare utvecklar Saab teknik och lösningar för en säkrare och mer hållbar värld.


Saab utvecklar, tillverkar och underhåller avancerade system inom flygteknik, vapen, ledningssystem, sensorer och undervattenssystem. Saab har sitt huvudkontor i Sverige men en global verksamhet där Saab är en del av många nationers försvarsförmåga. Läs mer om oss här
company_description: Saab serves the global market with world-leading products, services and solutions from military defense to civil security.

title: Cloud efficiency engineer
description:
We're looking for a cloud efficiency engineer to join the Performance Optimization Squad in Core Infra. This is a hands-on specialist role: you'll bring deep expertise in cloud infrastructure cost optimization and apply it directly to implementing multi-million-dollar efficiency gains across one of the world's largest Kubernetes fleets.
   

  

 You'll work alongside senior engineers and a data scientist who identify and prioritise opportunities. Your job is to take those opportunities and make them real — digging into GCP configurations, workload profiles, and Kubernetes resource settings to implement changes that reduce cost while maintaining performance and reliability. You bring the specialist knowledge that makes the difference between a safe 30% rightsizing and an aggressive one that pages on-call at 3am.
   

  

 The Opportunity
   

  

 Our client runs at a massive scale; the Performance Optimization Squad was formed to capture systematic efficiency gains that individual teams miss. The pipeline extends to JVM tuning, autoscaling efficiency, workload placement, and storage optimization.
   

  

 This role exists because cloud cost optimization at this scale requires specialist knowledge: understanding GCP pricing models, machine type trade-offs, committed use discount strategies, and how Kubernetes scheduling interacts with cloud billing. You'll bring that expertise to a team of strong infrastructure engineers who know the platform deeply but need a partner who thinks natively in cost-per-unit-of-compute.
   

  

 What We're Looking For
   

  

* 5+ years of experience in cloud infrastructure engineering, with hands-on work in cost optimization and resource efficiency (not just monitoring or reporting)
* Deep expertise in Google Cloud Platform (or equivalent depth in AWS/Azure with willingness to transfer to GCP) — you understand machine type characteristics, pricing models, and how Kubernetes scheduling decisions translate to cloud spend
* Hands-on experience optimizing Kubernetes/GKE workloads at scale — you've personally rightsized services, tuned autoscalers, and debugged resource contention issues
* Strong enough in Java to profile JVM-based services, interpret GC logs, adjust heap and thread pool configurations, and evaluate runtime alternatives — this squad's fleet is heavily JVM-based
* Demonstrated ability to implement cost optimizations that delivered measurable savings in a cloud-native environment
* Skilled at navigating the reliability trade-offs of efficiency work
* Deep JVM performance tuning experience is a plus


 Start/End: 2026-06-08 to 2026-12-07

title: Senior Infrastructure Engineer – Cloud
description:
**SENIOR INFRASTRUCTURE ENGINEER – CLOUD**
**Would you like to work in an international environment where you’ll make a difference every day? At Permobil, we’re looking for game-changers to join us as we innovate for individuals and develop the world’s most advanced assistive solutions.**
 At
 **Permobil** 
 , the people who use our products come first. Our purpose is to create advanced assistive solutions that make the lives of adults and children living with disabilities more enriching. Our people are passionate about pushing the boundaries of technology to deliver innovations that improve lives. We are a sustainable and responsible business committed to continuous improvement, and our customers can trust us to provide market-leading solutions driven by insights-based clinical data.
   

  

**Position Overview**
 We are seeking a
 **Senior Infrastructure Engineer - Cloud** 
 to help build, operate, and evolve our global hybrid cloud platform on Microsoft Azure. In this role, you will design and implement scalable, secure cloud infrastructure supporting business-critical applications and services across our global footprint. You will play a key role in areas such as Azure landing zones, governance, Azure Policy, hybrid connectivity, and Azure Arc capabilities, while collaborating closely with infrastructure, platform, and security teams to deliver reliable enterprise cloud services.
   

  

**Key Responsibilities**
* Design, deploy, and manage Azure hybrid cloud infrastructure
* Develop and maintain Azure landing zones and governance models
* Implement Azure Policy, RBAC, and Azure Arc capabilities
* Drive adoption of containers, AKS, and hybrid runtime solutions
* Manage Azure networking, connectivity, and security services
* Monitor cloud performance, costs, and optimization opportunities
* Implement observability solutions using Azure Monitor and Application Insights
* Manage secrets, certificates, and keys using Azure Key Vault
* Build and maintain Infrastructure as Code using Terraform, Bicep, or ARM
* Operate and improve CI/CD pipelines using Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions
* Evaluate new Azure services and provide technical guidance and documentation


**Qualifications & Skills**
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field. Relevant Azure certifications (AZ-104, AZ-305, AZ-500) are highly valued,
* 5+ years of hands on experience with Microsoft Azure infrastructure (networking, compute, storage, identity),
* Proven experience designing and evolving enterprise cloud platforms,
* Strong experience with hybrid cloud architectures, including Azure Arc and datacenter integration,
* Deep understanding of Azure networking (VNets, NSGs, private endpoints, ExpressRoute, Firewall, Application Gateway),
* Experience implementing governance and security controls (Azure Policy, RBAC, Entra ID, Key Vault),
* Solid experience with Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, Bicep, ARM),
* Experience supporting application and DevOps teams with cloud platforms, containers, or CI/CD,
* Fluent in English.


**Meriting Experience & Skills**
* Experience with containers and Kubernetes (AKS) in enterprise environments,
* Exposure to secondary cloud platforms (e.g., AWS) in a hybrid or multi cloud context,
* Experience with disaster recovery design in hybrid environments,
* Experience in regulated or global enterprise environments,
* Fluent in Swedish.


**Who are you?**
 You are a structured and analytical problem solver who thrives in complex hybrid cloud environments. You combine strong technical depth with clear communication, making it easy for both technical and non-technical stakeholders to understand and act on your guidance. Comfortable working across global teams and time zones, you bring a proactive and curious mindset, continuously looking for ways to improve platform maturity, standards, and overall cloud capability.
   

  

**Why is Permobil the next step for you?**
* You’ll make a difference. Every day. Everything we do leads to understanding and improving the lives of our users. Through our evidence-based innovation, we make a difference to people’s lives.
* You’ll make your mark as part of our future. We collaborate with colleagues across borders to Innovate for Individuals. The impact you make personally could lead change around the world.
* You’ll feel welcome from day one. We’re known for being great colleagues, who are collaborative, fun and at the cutting-edge. Everyone at Permobil cares as much as you do about making a positive difference.


**Application Process**
 We will conduct selection continuously so send us your application today or before
 **June 26th** 
 at the latest!
   

  

 Permobil is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We welcome applications from all qualified individuals.
   

  

 For more information about the position, please contact Erik Jensen, Manager Global Infrastructure, Erik.Jensen@permobil.com
   

  

 For information about the recruitment process, please contact Zivile Mitkute, Talent Acquisition Partner, zivile.mitkute@permobil.com
   

  

**Please respect that we do not wish recruitment assistance or advertising, we decline calls from recruitment and advertising providers.**
**More About Permobil**
 Permobil founder Dr. Per Uddén believed that helping people achieve the greatest level of independence is a basic human right and, for over 50 years, Permobil has held fast to that belief. Permobil is a global leader in advanced rehabilitation technology, passionate about better understanding our users’ needs and improving their quality of life through state-of-the-art healthcare solutions. Today, those solutions include power wheelchairs, seating and positioning products, power assist, and manual wheelchairs.
   

  

 Permobil is part of Patricia Industries, a subsidiary of Investor AB, and is headquartered in Sweden. Permobil has 2 000 team members in 18 countries around the world. For more information regarding the company’s storied history and complete product line, visit permobil.com.

title: Engineering Manager
description:
**WHO WE ARE** 





 At Trustly, we're building a smarter, faster, and more secure financial future by revolutionizing the world of payments. As a global leader in Open Banking Payments, we are establishing Pay by Bank as the new standard at checkout, providing unparalleled freedom, speed, and ease to millions of consumers and merchants worldwide.
 




  






 Our Ambition: To build the world’s most disruptive payment network and redefine what the payment experience should feel like.
 




  






 Trustly is a global team of innovators, collaborators, and doers.  If you are driven by a strong sense of purpose and thrive in a dynamic, entrepreneurial, and high-growth environment, join us and be part of a team that’s transforming the way the world pays.
 




  





**About the Product & Tech Organisation** 



  


  
 


  



 Our Product & Technology organisation drives the development of Trustly’s products. The cross functional teams collectively work with innovative ideas, development of products and platform and set the vision and strategy for the problem spaces they own. The Product & Tech organisation consists of a bit more than 200 people spread across Stockholm, Lisbon, Örebro, Paris, London and Izmir. 
 



  


  
 


  



 All teams consist of a product manager, an engineering manager, a lead developer and engineers. Some teams also have designers and analytics competence in the team. They all work closely together to enable new products and features within existing products within Europe and to make sure the payment platform runs without interruptions and with high performance.
 



  


  
 


  



 We are now looking to strengthen our engineering capacity within Product & Technology with one more senior position. We operate in an agile culture combined with DevOps, allowing our teams to take on full responsibility from code to on-call.
 



  


  
 


  


**About the Role** 



  


  
 


  



 As an
 
**Engineering Manager** 

 at Trustly, you will play a crucial role in leading and supporting an agile team of talented engineers. Working closely with product managers, lead developers, and other stakeholders, you’ll ensure delivery of high-quality, scalable, and reliable systems while fostering a positive and empowering team culture. This is an excellent opportunity to bridge technical excellence and people management, delivering real impact in the payments industry.
 





  




**What you'll do** 
  
* **Team Leadership** 
 : Lead, coach, and support a team of engineers, enabling them to perform at their best while fostering a culture of collaboration, ownership, and continuous learning
  
* **Delivery Excellence** 
 : Drive successful delivery of the team’s commitments, ensuring alignment with domain objectives and product goals
  
* **Technical Collaboration** 
 : Work hands-on with lead developers and engineers to design, implement, and maintain scalable systems and solutions aligned with Trustly’s technical standards
  
* **Product Partnership** 
 : Work closely with the product manager to define and prioritise the team’s roadmap, balancing short-term goals with long-term vision
  
* **Operational Ownership** 
 : Ensure your team’s systems operate reliably in production, with high performance, availability, and maintainability
  
* **Talent Development** 
 : Recruit, onboard, and mentor engineers, creating an environment where they can grow and thrive
  
* **Process Improvement** 
 : Advocate for and implement best practices in agile methodologies, DevOps, and engineering processes to continuously improve team effectiveness
  

  



  




**Who you are** 
  
* Proven experience managing engineering teams in agile environments, with a strong focus on team empowerment and delivery
  
* Hands-on engineering background with expertise in Java and experience in building scalable, robust systems
  
* Familiarity with modern tech stacks including Kubernetes, Docker, Kafka, and Spring Boot
  
* Experience working with cloud solutions (preferably AWS), monitoring, and analytics
  
* Knowledge of service-based architecture and microservices
  
* Strong ability to balance technical decisions with business needs, ensuring delivery without sacrificing scalability, performance, or reliability
  
* Committed to fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and excellence
  
* Passion for DevOps practices and ownership, with a track record of driving improvements in automation and monitoring
  
* Exceptional communication, problem-solving, and leadership skills
  
* Fluent in English
  

  



  




**At Trustly, you’ll have the opportunity to:** 
  
* Shape the future of payments as part of a global leader in the industry
  
* Work with cutting-edge technology and highly skilled colleagues in an agile and collaborative environment
  
* Take on a role with real impact, where your contributions directly drive the success of your team and Trustly’s mission
  
* Enjoy a supportive and dynamic culture that encourages growth, innovation, and ownership
  

  






  




  


**Our Fantastic Benefits**

 (varies by location)
 




 🌴
 **20 to 30 days of holiday** 
 to support a healthy work-life balance
 



 🥳
 **Monthly team outing allowance** 
 to enjoy social events with your colleagues
 



 👶
 **Parental leave top-up** 
 additional support for new parents
 



 🥐
 **Daily breakfast and on-site perks** 
 to make your workday smoother
 



 💚
 **Well being support** 
 our health allowance covers gym memberships, massages, and much more to help you feel your best
 



**PLUS** 
 additional benefits designed to enhance your work-life experience!
 



  





**Shape Your Role and Make an Impact**




 Join a dynamic environment where you can take ownership, drive change, and continuously embrace new challenges. We’re looking for dedicated and highly motivated individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment and enjoy collaborating across different areas of the organisation.
 



 If your skills and experience align with this role, we’d love to hear from you!
 **Apply now and submit your CV in English.** 



  


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Forward Deployed AI Accelerator
description:
**Global hiring runs on Teamtailor, the category leader trusted by thousands of companies and recruiters**
 Teamtailor is the hiring platform that powers company growth. Our end-to-end hiring platform powers 250,000 monthly active users across 12,000 customers in 90 countries worldwide. Our users hire more than 1 million people per year via our platform, representing ~1% of all hires in Europe and North America. Our mission is to continue building the global category leader for hiring people.
   

  

 Teamtailor is growing rapidly, currently at 600 people across 25 offices in 6 continents and scaling with hundreds of new customers per month. At the same time, we are undergoing a fundamental transformation in how we build the product and build the company - operating, organising and executing in new ways with use of AI.
   

  

 We work with AI in three layers: Building AI into the product, building the product with AI, and building the company with AI.
   

  

 Our goal with AI is to empower every single employee to accelerate what we do best - delivering exceptional experiences to our users, customers, and candidates.
   

  

 We offer a unique opportunity to be part of a transformational journey - building the next generation of how a fast-moving scale-up operates.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
 Teamtailor operates with three functions: Product, GTM and Operations. As a Forward Deployed AI Accelerator, you will be embedded within the GTM and Operations organisation to work alongside project teams and senior leaders to design, build and implement autonomous, multi-agent workflows.
   

  

 We will be intentional with how and where we apply AI. The idea is not to deploy AI everywhere, but to create leverage where it matters and enable our employees to do their best work.
   

  

 You will be part of the Corporate Development team that runs projects across the GTM and Operations organisation. You will also be part of a broader group that leads how we continue to build Teamtailor with AI and help craft our AI-native playbook.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Sit with teams in GTM and Operations to learn workflows deeply
* Identify and document the highest-leverage workflow transformations
* Help evaluate and build new tools, agents, automations and skills tailored to each workflow
* Coach and partner with each function through a progressive journey: from awareness, to first win, to regular AI integration, to full workflow transformation and self-sufficiency
* Process map and identify patterns across our organisation and systematically scale what works
* Document tools, playbook and transformation projects so more teams can access and use each other’s work
* Share wins within the organisation to build momentum, inspire and celebrate success


**Who You Are**
 You might be doing this today: building products as a full-stack developer, shipping internal tools as an AI engineer, running cross-functional projects in ops or strategy, automating workflows in RevOps, or advising companies on how to get value from AI. The common thread: you build things that change how people work.
   

  

* You are a deep AI practitioner. You build agents, automations and tools fluently. You have built things that changed how real work gets done. You can build in real time alongside the people you support.
* Deep knowledge in how a fast-moving scale-up operates and grows
* Bias toward action and speed. You'd rather show someone a working proof-of-concept on their own deliverable today than present a polished deck about what's theoretically possible next quarter
* You are a pattern recogniser. When you build something that works for one person, you immediately see how it applies to others. You think in systems, not one-off solutions
* Comfortable with ambiguity. This is a new team building a new operating model


**Minimum Requirements**
* 5+ years of professional experience in a role requiring analytical thinking, problem-solving and cross-functional collaboration
* Demonstrated hands-on experience building AI-powered tools, agents, automations or workflows that transformed real processes
* Track record of coaching, teaching or enabling others (formally or informally)
* Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences
* Comfort working across multiple domains, work streams and relationships simultaneously


**Preferred Qualifications**
* Proficiency with AI development tools and platforms (Claude, Claude Code, Codex, custom agent frameworks, workflow automation platforms, API integrations)
* Experience building and scaling internal tools, playbooks or templates that were adopted beyond your immediate team
* Experience with change management, organizational transformation, or large-scale enablement programs

title: Software Architect
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger- and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal, in 2018 - and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco - we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**The Role**
 As a
 **Software Architect / Solutions Architect** 
 at Air Apps, you will be responsible for
 **defining and overseeing the overall system architecture** 
 for our AI-powered platform. You will collaborate with engineering teams, product managers, and stakeholders to design
 **scalable, secure, and high-performing solutions** 
 that align with business objectives.
   

  

 You will play a key role in shaping our
 **technology strategy, evaluating new tools and frameworks, and ensuring best practices** 
 in software design, cloud infrastructure, and system integration.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Define and maintain the overall architecture and system design for scalable and efficient software solutions.
* Provide technical leadership to engineering teams, guiding them in best practices for architecture, coding, and system scalability.
* Design and document high-level system diagrams, data flows, and API structures.
* Evaluate and recommend technologies, frameworks, and cloud services to optimize performance, security, and cost-efficiency.
* Ensure high availability, fault tolerance, and security in all architectural decisions.
* Oversee system integrations, including microservices, APIs, and third-party services.
* Work closely with DevOps and SRE teams to ensure smooth deployment and operational reliability.
* Establish coding and architecture standards, ensuring maintainability and scalability of codebases.
* Identify and mitigate technical risks, ensuring system resilience and future scalability.
* Conduct architecture reviews and performance audits to continuously improve system efficiency.


**Requirements**
* Around 6+ years of experience in software development and architecture.
* Strong expertise in system design, cloud computing, and microservices architecture.
* Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform) and cloud-native architectures.
* Proficiency in backend technologies (e.g., Node.js, Python, Java, .NET) and experience with modern frontend frameworks (e.g., React, Angular, Vue).
* Knowledge of databases (SQL and NoSQL), caching strategies, and data pipelines.
* Experience with event-driven architectures, API design, and containerization (Docker, Kubernetes).
* Strong understanding of security best practices, identity management, and encryption techniques.
* Ability to evaluate trade-offs in architectural decisions, balancing scalability, cost, and complexity.
* Familiarity with DevOps, CI/CD pipelines, and Infrastructure as Code (Terraform, CloudFormation).
* Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex architectural concepts to technical and non-technical stakeholders.


**What benefits do we offer?**
* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

**Application Disclaimer**
 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.

title: Senior VMware-konsult
description:
Till ett uppdrag hos vår kund i Stockholm söker vi en Senior VMware-konsult som ska ansvara för att designa, vidareutveckla och säkerställa en stabil, säker och effektiv virtualiserings- och molninfrastruktur. Du arbetar med hela livscykeln för IT-infrastruktur; från arkitektur och implementation till drift, automation och kontinuerlig förbättring.
 



  





 Som konsult på Eccera får du en trygg anställning och samlar på dig värdefull arbetslivserfarenhet på uppdrag hos våra kunder. Din konsultchef stöttar dig i din utveckling där du hela tiden fyller på med nya erfarenheter och kunskaper. En utmärkt möjlighet att göra karriär.
 



  





**Om rollen** 




 Rollen passar dig som har minst 5 års djupgående erfarenhet av VMware, kombinerat med flera års tidigare arbete inom IT-infrastruktur, exempelvis inom drift, systemadministration eller datacenter. Du har ett starkt helhetsperspektiv och trivs i miljöer där automation, skalbarhet och stabilitet är affärskritiska.
 



  





 Du ansvarar för VMware-plattformens centrala komponenter och arbetar aktivt med orkestrering, självbetjäning och standardisering genom moderna verktyg och arbetssätt. Du blir en nyckelperson i uppgraderingar, migreringar och vidareutveckling av infrastrukturen, samt samarbetar tätt med nätverk, säkerhet, applikation och andra tekniska team.
 



  





**Dina arbetsuppgifter** 



* Designa, administrera och optimera VMware-miljöer i komplexa IT-landskap
* Arbeta med automation och orkestrering av IT-infrastruktur
* Implementera och vidareutveckla lösningar baserade på VMware Aria Automation och Aria Orchestrator
* Ansvara för virtualiserade nätverkslösningar med VMware NSX-T
* Arbeta med VMware vCloud Director i leverans- och tjänsteorienterade miljöer
* Säkerställa kontinuitet och återställning med VMware SRM / Live Recovery
* Planera och genomföra uppgraderingar, migreringar och förbättringsinitiativ'
* Automatisera drift och leverans med hjälp av script och versionshantering
* Samarbeta med andra team och fungera som teknisk expert och rådgivare



  





**Din profil** 




  





*Krav:* 



* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av arbete med VMware i större eller affärskritiska miljöer
* Tidigare flerårig erfarenhet av arbete inom IT-infrastruktur eller systemdrift
* Mycket goda kunskaper inom automation och orkestrering av IT-infrastruktur
* Mycket goda kunskaper inom:



 - VMware Aria Automation
 



 - VMware Aria Orchestrator
 



 - VMware vCloud Director
 



 - VMware NSX-T
 



 - VMware SRM / Live Recovery
 


* Goda kunskaper i Git (GitHub/GitLab)
* Goda kunskaper i PowerShell
* Goda kunskaper i JavaScript
* Mycket god kommunikationsförmåga på svenska och engelska



  





*Meriterande:* 



* Erfarenhet av större datacenter- eller molnmiljöer
* Certifieringar inom VMware (t.ex. VCP, VCAP)
* Erfarenhet av CI/CD-kopplingar mot infrastruktur
* Vana att arbeta enligt DevOps- eller plattformsprinciper



  





**Vad vi erbjuder** 




 På Eccera får du de verktyg du behöver för att nå dina mål. Vi erbjuder alla våra konsulter kvalificerade utbildningar och certifieringar inom den senaste tekniken. Vi sätter stort värde på att våra medarbetare hela tiden ska fortsätta att utvecklas. Därför erbjuder vi dig möjligheten att lägga upp en individuell utbildningsplan, där vi bekostar såväl studiematerial som själva certifieringarna. Under din utveckling kommer vi matcha din kompetens mot rätt uppdrag i takt med att du avancerar.
